evals.prompt: Initialize memory in this workspace. I want MEMORY.md, memory/, and TASKS.md, but keep it lightweight and ask me about unknown project shorthand before inventing meanings.
evals.expected_output: Creates or proposes the correct memory/task file structure, uses MEMORY.md rather than CLAUDE.md, and asks for clarification instead of guessing.
assertions: Creates MEMORY.md with People, Terms, Projects, and Preferences sections.
Creates memory/glossary.md plus memory/people, memory/projects, and memory/context directories.
Creates TASKS.md with Active, Waiting On, Someday, and Done sections.

evals.prompt: Remember that PSR means Pipeline Status Report, Phoenix is our active database migration, and Todd is Todd Martinez from Finance. Then add a task to ask Todd for the Phoenix PSR by Friday.
evals.expected_output: Stores terms, project, and person in the right memory locations and adds a TASKS.md item with decoded context.
assertions: Creates memory/people/todd-martinez.md or an equivalent lowercase kebab-case person profile.
Creates memory/projects/project-phoenix.md or an equivalent lowercase kebab-case project profile.
Adds an Active TASKS.md item for asking Todd about the Phoenix PSR.

evals.prompt: Run an update: check TASKS.md for stale items, find unknown acronyms in active tasks, and move anything no longer active out of MEMORY.md but keep the details in memory/.
evals.expected_output: Uses the update workflow, reads current state first, triages tasks, identifies memory gaps, and demotes stale hot-cache entries without deleting durable history.
assertions: Reads existing MEMORY.md and TASKS.md before proposing changes.
Flags the unknown acronym QBR for clarification instead of inventing a meaning.
Suggests demoting stale Horizon hot-cache context from MEMORY.md while preserving memory/projects/project-horizon.md.
Does not delete durable memory files without confirmation.
